info.title: AS Status API
info.description: Creates and retrieves status feeds and events.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Creates status feeds and retrieves status objects and events.

post.summary: Creates a status feed and retrieves the first page of status objects.
post.description: Creates a status feed and retrieves the first page of status objects. The status feed will expire in 24 hours upon creation. Subsequent pages can be retrieved by calls to the GET /status-feed/{feed_id} endpoint.

parameters.name: Schwab-Resource-Version
parameters.description: The requested API version. Older versions are deprecated and may be retired in future releases.

get.summary: Retrieves status objects and their events in a status feed.
get.description: Retrieves status objects and their events in a status feed. Requests for the same feed return the next page of results using stateful server-side pagination. After all pages have been retrieved, further calls will consistently return the final page of results.

parameters.name: feed_id
parameters.description: Passes the unique identifier of the status feed.

parameters.name: page[limit]
parameters.description: The number of records to be returned. The default and maximum value is 1000. The value passed must remain the same as the initial call to the POST /status-feed endpoint.

parameters.name: showAccount
parameters.description:
Specifies how account numbers are displayed in the response. The values are:
`Mask`: Masks the account number as '*****6789'.
`Show`: Shows the account number.

get.summary: Retrieves events of a status object in a status feed.
get.description: Retrieves events of a status object in a status feed.

parameters.name: feed_id
parameters.description: Passes the unique identifier of the status feed.

parameters.name: object_id
parameters.description: Passes the unique identifier of the status object.

AccessToken.description: The **OAuth** token associated with the advisor.

HeaderCorrelId.name: Schwab-Client-CorrelId
HeaderCorrelId.description: The Correlation ID is a GUID that can be used to track a service call if support is needed.

MasterAccounts.description: Passes the master accounts. Requires `accounts` not be passed. Exclude the field to create a firm-level status feed.

Accounts.description: Passes the accounts. Requires `masterAccounts` not be passed. Exclude the field to create a firm-level status feed.

StartDate.description: Filters status objects created on or after the passed date (EST). The default value is 10 days prior. The earliest date supported is 90 days prior.

EndDate.description: Filters status objects created on or before the passed date (EST). The default value is the current date.

TimeFrame.description: The field by which to filter status objects via `startDate` and `endDate`.

Categories.description: Filters status objects by the category. All are returned if the filter is not passed.

MyqCaseId.description: Filters status objects by the unique identifier generated by Schwab processing systems for the work request.

ServiceRequestConfirmationId.description: Filters status objects by the Service Request Confirmation ID.

ActionCenterEnvelopeId.description: Filters status objects by the Action Center Envelope ID.

IncludeAllEvents.description: Indicates whether all or only the most recent events should be returned.

ShowAccount.description:
Specifies how account numbers are displayed in the response. The values are:
`Mask`: Masks the account number as '*****6789'.
`Show`: Shows the account number.

pageLimit.description: The number of records to be returned. The default and maximum value is 1000. The value passed must remain the same for subsequent calls to the GET /status-feed/{feed_id} endpoint.

StartDateV1.description: Filters status objects created on or after the passed date (EST). The earliest and default date supported is 90 days prior.

TimeFrameV1.description: The field by which to filter status objects via `startDate` and `endDate`.

firstPageOnly.description: Indicates whether 1000 (when set to true) or 2000 events should be returned.

id.description: The unique identifier of the status feed.

type.description: The type of the object returned.

actual.description: The actual number of status objects retrieved.

total.description: The total number of status objects in the status feed.

timestamp.description: The date and time (EST) of the request.

formattedTimestamp.description: The formatted date and time (EST) of the request.

timestampFilename.description: The date and time (EST) of the request formatted for a filename.

statusObjectId.description: The unique identifier of the status object.

processId.description: The unique identifier of the business process.

bundleId.description: The unique identifier of the bundle.

createdDate.description: The date and time (EST) when the object was created.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date and time (EST) when the object was last updated.

closedDate.description: The date and time (EST) when the request was closed.

source.description: The system that published the event.

sourceId.description: The unique identifier of the source.

sourceUser.description: The user or service account that published the event within the source.

tags.description: The labels that can be applied to status objects at a publishing time.

description.description: The description of the business process.

accountName.description: The first line of the account name.

accountRegistrationDetails.description: The second and third lines of the account name.

myqCaseId.description: The unique identifier generated by Schwab processing systems for the work request.

serviceRequestConfirmationId.description: The Service Request Confirmation ID sent after successful service request submission.

actionCenterEnvelopeId.description: The Action Center Envelope ID to action the work request.

isUpdatable.description: Indicates whether the request is updatable.

isConfidential.description: Indicates whether the account is confidential or restricted.

confidentialInfo.description: The access restrictions on the status object.

id.description: The unique identifier of the status event.

type.description: The type of the object returned.

profileType.description: The type of the client profile.

profileId.description: The unique identifier of the client profile.

auxProfileId.description: The unique identifier of the Aux (sub) profile.

confidentialDecisionSystem.description: The decision system used to determine confidentiality.

additionalInfoId.description: The unique identifier of additional info.

additionalInfoSystem.description: The system responsible for providing additional info.

additionalInfoType.description: The MIME type of additional information (e.g., text/html for links, text/plain for normal key-value pairs, application/json for JSON objects, or text/pdf for PDF files on SPOS).

canBeDeleted.description: Indicates whether additional info can be deleted.

channel.description: The channel which created the status object.

tags.description: The labels that can be applied to status objects at a publishing time.

id.description: The unique identifier of the status object.

type.description: The type of the object returned.

id.description: The unique identifier of the event.

type.description: The type of the object returned.

statusObjectId.description: The unique identifier of the status object.

status.description: The lifecycle stage of the status object.

currentStatus.description: The short summary of the step in the business process.

currentStatusMessageDetail.description: The message on the status of the request.

createdDate.description: The date and time (EST) when the request was created.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date and time (EST) when the request was last updated.

estimatedCompletionDate.description: The date and time (EST) by which the request is estimated to be completed.

assignmentGroup.description: The assignee to act on the status event in the "Action Needed" state.

source.description: The system that published the event.

sourceId.description: The unique identifier of the source.

sourceUser.description: The user or the service account that published the event within the source.

canBeDeleted.description: Indicates whether the status event can be deleted.

actualQuantity.description: The actual number of shares disclosed and nondisclosed.

shareAmount.description: The total price for the quantity of shares purchased.

longShort.description: Indicates whether the position is a short or a long.

wasTransferred.description: Indicates whether the asset was successfully transferred.

masterAccount.description: Passes the master account. Either `masterAccount` or `account` must be passed.

account.description: Passes the account. Either `masterAccount` or `account` must be passed.

myqCaseId.description: Passes the Schwab Case ID of an existing request for which to create the status event in.

message.description: The message of the status event. Either `message` or `documents` must be passed.
message.example: Have issues with John Doe account. Need it fixed.

documents.description: The documents to upload. A maximum of 10 files can be passed at once. The maximum size limit for all files is 30 MB. Either `message` or `documents` must be passed.

showAccount.description:
Specifies how account numbers are displayed in the response. The values are:
`Mask`: Masks the account number as '*****6789'.
`Show`: Shows the account number.

base64EncodedFileContent.description: The document as a Base64-encoded string.

type.description: The type of the object returned.

myqCaseId.description: The Schwab Case ID of the request the status event was created in.

name.description: The title of the status event.

message.description: The message of the status event.
message.example: Have issues with John Doe account. Need it fixed.

invalidAccounts.description: The accounts which are invalid or for which the user is not authorized.

invalidMasterAccounts.description: The master accounts which are invalid or for which the user is not authorized.

FormattedAccount.description: The unique identifier of the client account.

FormattedMasterAccount.description: The unique identifier of the master account.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this occurrence of the error.

title.description: The short title describing the category of the error.

detail.description: The error description associated with this occurrence of the error. Provides details of the error. The actual error may vary.

ErrorInfoSource.description: The optional extended error info pointing to the source that triggered the error.

pointer.description: The JSON pointer (RFC6901) to the associated entity in the request document (e.g., "/data" for a primary data object or "/data/attributes/{property name}" for a specific attribute).

parameter.description: The input parameter which caused the error.

header.description: The request header which caused the error.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this occurrence of the error.

title.description: The short title describing the category of the error.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this occurrence of the error.

title.description: The short title describing the category of the error.

SchwabClientCorrelId.description: The Correlation ID is a GUID that can be used to track a service call if support is needed.
